human_verification.notes: Reviewed the construction-and-field-services safety-compliance package for boundary quality, authority-source quality, permit and inspection gate handling, closeout dependency coverage, multi-employer responsibility handling, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to construction and field-services safety-compliance support, permit and inspection dependency awareness, evidence discipline, and closeout blocker handling.

Authority sources cover OSHA construction rules, OSHA recordkeeping, OSHA inspection evidence handling, multi-employer responsibility, public permitting workflow, public closeout workflow, and recognized federal construction operating standards with refresh and decay rules.

Evaluation scenarios cover missing evidence, permit and site-readiness gaps, inspection and closeout blockers, changed site conditions, policy conflicts, code-interpretation refusal, regulator-facing refusal, and field-service work-order handling.
The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/construction-and-field-services/safety-compliance-specialist/ to keep this overlay in the canonical NAICS-derived path.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around permit approval, code interpretation, licensed trade signoff, legal interpretation, and regulator-facing work.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated construction and field-services safety-compliance overlay where legal, permit, inspection, and licensed-trade authority remain external.

The specialist is intentionally collaboration-oriented: it knows when to hand work to permitting, inspection, superintendent, project-controls, safety leadership, legal, engineering, and licensed-trade lanes.
